body.notes: Participation in IETF meetings constitutes agreement to the Note Well, which permits recording, transcription, and public archival of proceedings. See also BCP 78 (RFC 5378) and BCP 79 (RFC 8179) for IPR policies.

nature of being able to have links that have formats formats that can then have links you it it just feels like you're not going to be able to get away from the potential of deeply nested stuff I clunky well I mean you would immediately face the question of how you would serialize that in an HTTP header fair enough which you know is a well-known little mindfield okay any other questions on the topic document thank you Mark thank you uh rest API media types this was a split off from a general or two uh registrations uh document um started out with a flurry of open issues when the thing was created 2022 now that's more than a year ago um we brought out an additional author there hasn't been a lot of work um it sort of feels like we should do it since we split the thing off one of them out um so we'll ask on the list or first we'll speak to the authors and see if they're still willing to pursue and then we'll ask on the list um I don't think any of the issues were particularly intractable do you darl yeah no and and the most recent revision I believe Roberto split open API out because I think a lot of the challenges were around Jason schema and uh dealing with fragments in Jason schema and and uh we the only blocking issue around um uh the open API specification was uh security considerations there is now a security considerations doc that has been written in the open API repo um so we have something to reference there um the only other issue that is um a question is at the moment the media type registration suggests creating open API application openapi plus Json and application slopen API plus yaml but it doesn't suggest registering application open API and uh that especially because of all the conversations going on about multiple suffixes and the processing of multiple suffixes I'm sort of feeling like we should um register application slj justop API which will assume that it is which can be processed by a yaml processor because Json is a subset of yaml and so if you if you receive application openen API you can just throw it into a a yaml process and it'll work regardless of what you get so that's that's a followup that uh I want to do with Roberto okay um yeah I'll I'll point out Roberto told us a two years ago that he was getting a new job with different responsibilities so that's why he's not been participating much although he did a really nice job helping to finish off some of his documents okay so we'll check with the authors and ask on a list uh look for volunteers uh depr deprecation not depreciation um we're probably going to be looking for a volunteer to help finish this one this is much less work has many fewer issues um should just be since we had done the big one which is we're not going to tackle a whole life cycle of apis we're just going to be able to indicate to a client this API is going to go away in a time frame um so it should be pretty easy to help finish this off if someone is looking to you know make their mark on and helping auor an RFC uh contact HTTP API D chairs um but uh again we'll check with the authors check on the list ien poy I I whatever that word is um this was in working group last call um Mark identified a handful of issues that really were blockers before proceeding uh it needs a revision Daryl's been working with the authors they will we will we will redo the working group last call um second time the charm uh anything you want to say about it Daryl uh just that I'm was pleasantly surprised that there were a number of other uh people who've got in recently and commented on some of the issues that are there especially with people who have um experience of implement implementing um and some of the questions that were raised have had feedback so uh there's some good helpful conversations going on in that set of issues now yeah just to to point out we have a Gateway weekly message weekly weekly message summary um between the GitHub issues that have got had comments and and the mailing list but given the community and its orientation that when we started half the people were not ITF iters um a lot of the discussion on these things is the uh is on GitHub so uh you may want to look there and feel free to comment there um if you go to our data tracker page there's a resource that points to the project and then there's all the individual repos one for each document so uh as a reminder just consider looking on GitHub or post something to the mailing list also is is legit uh bite range uh Austin is online remote from Arizona I think in the US uh he just published a new addition version of the document um and anything you want to say about it Austin yeah uh I mean briefly um yeah sorry I was traveling for the past month so there might be emails and things from people that I uh have still have to reply to um but the new version um uh and just to refresh bite range lets you like overwrite specific regions of a Target file um which is sort of the opposite of getting partial content um the this revision has just a new syntax for the content offset header um previously it was sort of overloading the content uh range header which was suspicious at best um so it uses uh the structured fields which I i' likeed someone to review um and maybe bike shed some of the parameter names um and I'm just noting I'm interested in keeping the the scope somewhat restricted to publishing a media type because the media type does useful things and then if there's other use cases that um grow on that um maybe those should be looked at separately and I'm aware of two first is for sort of operational transform like use cases um like we talked about with braid last session where they would like to do search and replace or splicing um which is not overriding but it would probably be a similar file format for what they're looking to do and then also resumable uploads is interested in a feature almost exactly like this um but would prefer to not have to parse a new media type it would you know for efficiency purposes or whatever um maybe baking that into the protocol or just minting a header that must be R and um that would also have to be separately looked at um to make sure that the server doesn't uh Mis accept the request when it would uh not be understood or um if you know what I mean so yeah um that's pretty much all there is to that yeah okay and that last part was talking about that addresses the maybe split some things into the HTTP protocol is that what you meant yeah um I mean let me rephrase that yeah uh if the one of the goals or like especially for resumable uploads one of the ideal would be that we don't have to um re like pars anything from the request body the request just is a Content that's going to be appended to the document or overwriting the document and then any of the other metadata that specifies where in the document that goes that would be part of an HTTP header or something that's actually already in the htttp syntax um that would definitely be probably more of an HTTP Biz or working group question rather than apis um and that would require some thinking about uh making sure that the server Rejects requests if it doesn't support that functionality um which could be either like a new HTTP method or maybe just a special case reserved name for a media type parameter uh using the patch method um there's a bunch of different ways which would have to be examined but that's sort of outside the scope of this document yes okay all right thank you thanks for staying up uh any of business does anyone have any IDE ideas concerns problems propos new documents that they would like to bring to this group um we have managed to work through a large portion of our backlog we're now
